record.field: Flagsmith is an open-source feature flag and remote configuration platform that lets teams ship, control, and roll back features without redeploying code.

Flagsmith is an open-source feature flag and remote configuration platform that lets teams ship, control, and roll back features without redeploying code.

It wraps sections of your application in feature flags that you toggle per environment, user, or segment from a central dashboard. Teams use it to decouple deployment from release, run staged rollouts and A/B tests, and manage remote config across web, mobile, and server-side applications. It ships with 15+ official SDKs and a documented REST API, and can be self-hosted for full control over data and security policies.

Key features
  • Boolean feature flags and typed remote config values managed per environment
  • User and segment targeting with attribute-based rules and percentage rollouts
  • Multivariate flags for A/B/n testing and gradual feature exposure
  • Server-side and edge flag evaluation with official SDKs for 15+ languages and frameworks
  • REST API and webhooks for automation and CI/CD integration
  • Self-hosting via Docker Compose with full control over data residency and security
Best for

Engineering and product teams that want trunk-based development, safe progressive rollouts, and centralized remote configuration, and organizations with data-sensitivity or compliance needs that require keeping feature-flag infrastructure on-premises or in their own cloud.

services: postgres: image: postgres:15.5-alpine environment: POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DATABASE_PASSWORD} POSTGRES_DB: flagsmith volumes: - ./pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -d flagsmith -U postgres interval: 2s timeout: 2s retries: 20 start_period: 20s container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postgres' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal flagsmith: image: docker.flagsmith.com/flagsmith/flagsmith:2.177.1 environment: DATABASE_URL: postgresql://postgres:${DATABASE_PASSWORD}@postgres:5432/flagsmith USE_POSTGRES_FOR_ANALYTICS: 'true' ENVIRONMENT: production DJANGO_ALLOWED_HOSTS: '*' FLAGSMITH_DOMAIN: ${FLAGSMITH_DOMAIN:-localhost:8000} DJANGO_SECRET_KEY: ${DJANGO_SECRET_KEY} TASK_RUN_METHOD: TASK_PROCESSOR PROMETHEUS_ENABLED: 'true' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8000' depends_on: postgres: condition: service_healthy container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network flagsmith-task-processor: image: docker.flagsmith.com/flagsmith/flagsmith:2.177.1 environment: DATABASE_URL: postgresql://postgres:${DATABASE_PASSWORD}@postgres:5432/flagsmith USE_POSTGRES_FOR_ANALYTICS: 'true' DJANGO_ALLOWED_HOSTS: '*' PROMETHEUS_ENABLED: 'true' ports: - 8000 depends_on: - flagsmith command: run-task-processor container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_flagsmith_task_processor' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR. # Si POSTGRES_USER/POSTGRES_DB vienen vacíos (la imagen usa 'postgres' por defecto), # caemos a 'postgres': con $POSTGRES_USER vacío pg_dump intentaría el rol "root" del SO # y fallaría con «role "root" does not exist». docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "${POSTGRES_USER:-postgres}" -d "${POSTGRES_DB:-${POSTGRES_USER:-postgres}}" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura (mismo default a 'postgres') docker compose up -d postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "${POSTGRES_USER:-postgres}"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "${POSTGRES_USER:-postgres}" -d "${POSTGRES_DB:-${POSTGRES_USER:-postgres}}" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c "psql -U \${POSTGRES_USER:-postgres} -d \${POSTGRES_DB:-postgres} -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
